title: Confinement toolkits — per-tool OS-sandbox allowances, classified by boundary effect
description: Formal shape of confinement-toolkit.yaml — a registry of named bundles of OS-sandbox allowances a tool needs to work under confinement (per ADR-008). Each allowance is classified `narrowing` (makes the box usable, no reach increase — auto-applicable) or `widening` (carves reach out of the box — explicit-gesture-only). Companion per COR-018 / the .pkit/schemas/ area conventions.

toolkits.description: Toolkits. Keys are kebab-case tool/tool-group ids (uv, gh, docker, …); values match $defs.entry.

confinement-toolkit_ref.description: Narrowed cross-schema reference pattern for this namespace (per COR-019). Used by permission-profile.recommended_accommodations and permission-config.confinement_accommodations to name a toolkit.

description.description: One line: what the tool is and why it needs out-of-box reach.

detect.description: Repo-relative glob patterns whose presence signals this tool is in use (e.g. `uv.lock`, `.github/`). Consumed by `sandbox accommodate --detect`. Detection proposes NARROWING allowances only (ADR-008 rule 3); a toolkit whose allowances are all widening is never auto-proposed.

allowances.description: The sandbox-block allowances this tool needs, each classified by boundary effect.

kind.description: Which sandbox-block key this writes: allow-write/allow-read → filesystem.allowWrite/allowRead; allow-unix-socket → network.allowUnixSockets; exclude-command → excludedCommands; weaker-tls → enableWeakerNetworkIsolation.

effect.description: The load-bearing classification (ADR-008). `narrowing` = makes the box usable without enlarging reach (allow-write of a build cache, a needed socket) — auto-applicable, detectable, committable. `widening` = lowers the boundary (a command carved out of the box, weakened TLS) — explicit per-invocation gesture only, never committed, never detected, never applied by setup.

value.description: The path / command / socket the allowance targets (e.g. `~/.cache/uv`, `gh`, `~/.1password/agent.sock`). Omitted for `weaker-tls`, which is a boolean toggle with no target.

note.description: Optional honesty gloss surfaced by `sandbox toolkit show` — especially for widening allowances (what reach it opens; that egress is not a security boundary, per ADR-004).
